seoTitle: How to Close QuickBooks at Year End
metaDescription: Learn how to close your books at year end in QuickBooks so your financial data is locked in and ready for tax filing or an audit.
intro: Closing the books at year end protects your finalized numbers from accidental changes and sets you up cleanly for tax season. This guide covers the essential steps to close QuickBooks for the year.

steps.heading: Reconcile all accounts
steps.body: Make sure every bank and credit card account is fully reconciled through the end of the year before closing.

steps.heading: Review your financial statements
steps.body: Check your profit and loss and balance sheet for the year to catch any errors before locking the period.

steps.heading: Set the closing date
steps.body: Go to your account settings and set a closing date for the fiscal year that's ending.

steps.heading: Add a closing date password
steps.body: Set a password on the closing date so changes to prior-year transactions require extra confirmation.

faq.question: Does setting a closing date prevent all changes to past transactions?
faq.answer: It doesn't fully prevent changes, but it requires a warning and often a password before someone can edit transactions before the closing date.

faq.question: Do I need my accountant involved in year-end close?
faq.answer: It's common practice to have an accountant review your books before officially closing the year, especially for tax purposes.

faq.question: Can I reopen the books after closing?
faq.answer: Yes, you can adjust or remove the closing date later if corrections are needed, though it's best to avoid frequent changes.

faq.question: What reports should I run before closing the year?
faq.answer: A profit and loss statement, balance sheet, and reconciliation reports for all accounts are commonly reviewed before closing.
